project.title: The production of Excretory-Secretory molecules from Heligmosomoides polygyrus bakeri fourth stage larvae varies between mixed and single sex cultures.
project.projectDescription: In this study, the composition of ES of male and female L4 stage Heligmosomoides polygyrus bakeri in the presence (cultured together) or absence (cultured alone) of the opposite sex was examined using mass spectrometry.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The mice were orally infected with 300 infective larvae (L3) H. polygyrus bakeri, then sacrificed six days after infection. The larvae were collected and then immediately used for ES preparation.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The acquired MS/MS data were preprocessed with Mascot Distiller software and a search was performed with the Mascot Search Engine. The significance threshold was set at p<0.01.

references.referenceLine: Maruszewska-Cheruiyot M, Szewczak L, Krawczak-Wójcik K, Głaczyńska M, Donskow-Łysoniewska K. The production of excretory-secretory molecules from Heligmosomoides polygyrus bakeri fourth stage larvae varies between mixed and single sex cultures. Parasit Vectors. 2021 14(1):106

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: Two independent experiments were conducted on ES obtained from nematode culture collected from independently infected mice.
Characteristics[SamplingTime]: The first 24 h collection of ES was discarded; medium was supplied and harvested for the next 72 h.
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Elite mass spectrometer (Thermo Electron Corp., San Jose, CA, USA)
Comment[Separation]: nano-HPLC RP-18 column (internal diameter 75 µM, Waters, Milford, MA, USA)
